title: Dianthera humifusa
taxonomy: Dianthera humifusa is classified within the family Acanthaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus family. This plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions. It belongs to the order Lamiales. The family Acanthaceae is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Dianthera humifusa being part of the tribe Justicieae. The classification of Dianthera humifusa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Acanthaceae family.
uses: Dianthera humifusa is a plant traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various ailments. It is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling.
